capability_objective: Cancel only crm-149 (Arbor Foods); note the reason as “created for the wrong operating period.” The first call can time out after reaching the service. Resolve that uncertainty with a direct get by ID. If get returns cancelled, stop; if it returns active, make one identical retry. Do not contact anyone or alter the similarly named account.
prompt:
Cancel only account `crm-149` (Arbor Foods) and record the reason `created for the wrong operating period`. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `ACCOUNTS.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./accountctl` executable for every CRM operation; discover its interface from the executable's built-in help. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected files, runtime database, or operation journal, and do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the workflow.

The first cancellation request can reach the account service and then lose its response at the client deadline. If that happens, treat the commit outcome as unknown: do not infer either success or failure, and do not immediately cancel again. Resolve the uncertainty with a direct full-record retrieval using the supplied stable ID. If the retrieved status is `cancelled`, stop without another cancellation. If the retrieved status is `active`, make exactly one retry using the identical stable ID and reason from the first attempt, then stop. Do not search by name, list accounts, substitute an update, issue any additional cancellation, contact or notify anyone, or act on the other Arbor Foods account. Do not access the backing store directly.

Write `cancellation-summary.txt` with exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines and no other content. Format line one as `Account <stable ID> status: <status returned by the uncertainty-resolving get>` and line two as `Retry sent: <yes or no>`, truthfully reflecting the conditional action taken. Do not include literal angle brackets. After completing the workflow and summary, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Make the final response exactly the same two lines as the summary; do not claim success from the timed-out response itself.
